Prerequisites: Azure CLI already logged in (az account show must succeed)
Docker daemon running and accessible (docker version must not report daemon connection errors)
Sufficient Azure quota in the target region for Container Apps, Container Registry, Azure OpenAI, and Azure Monitor

Steps.Description: Create a new Azure Developer CLI environment
Set Azure subscription for the azd environment
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID $(az account show --query id -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Set Azure tenant for the azd environment
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_TENANT_ID $(az account show --query tenantId -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Relocate node_modules to repo-local cache directory (robust loop without nested quoting issues)
Steps.Command: bash -lc 'set -e; mkdir -p .cache; find . -maxdepth 5 -name package.json ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.git/*" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d "" pj; do d=$(dirname "$pj"); slug=${d#./}; slug=${slug//\//_}; [ -z "$slug" ] && slug=root; tgt=".cache/nm-$slug"; mkdir -p "$tgt"; rm -rf "$d/node_modules"; ln -sfn "$(pwd)/$tgt" "$d/node_modules"; echo "  -> $d/node_modules -> $tgt"; done'

Steps.Description: Exclude node_modules symlinks and cache from Docker build context to prevent COPY conflicts
Remove node_modules symlinks before Docker build to avoid BuildKit COPY directory/file conflict
Provision Azure resources and deploy all services using Azure Developer CLI
Ensure container images are built and pushed (azd deploy – idempotent verification after azd up)
